betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Guide: Recycling Depleted Fuel§f

§aCool your spent fuel rods, then recycle them for valuable materials!§f

When a nuclear reactor depletes its fuel, the rods become hot, pyrophoric, and highly radioactive. They must be cooled before recycling.

§6Cooling Process§f:
§b1.§f Depleted rods are automatically removed from large reactors
§b2.§f Extract via empty rods or waste ejector with adjacent crate/chest
§b3.§f Place in a §bSpent Fuel Pool Drum§f (holds 12 pieces) — requires water (natural body or cooling tower)
§b4.§f Each piece takes ~5 minutes to cool
§b5.§f Wear fire proximity suit or fire resistance potion when handling
§b6.§f Can be fully automated with Thermal Dynamics pipes

§6Recycling§f (§bCentrifuge§f) — after cooling:
§b• Depleted Uranium§f → 2× Tiny Nuclear Waste + 1× U-238 Nugget + 2× Reactor-Grade Pu Nuggets + 1× Tc-99 Nugget
§b• Uranium nugget§f — reusable for fuel
§b• Reactor-grade Plutonium§f — used for Plutonium or MOX fuel
§b• Technetium-99§f — used for Technetium Steel

§6Waste Disposal§f:
§b• Tiny piles of nuclear waste§f must be disposed of or utilized unconventionally
§b• RBMK waste§f can be used in the Radiation-Powered Engine

§eTip§f: Never handle hot depleted fuel without protection — it will burn you alive! Automate the full cooling→recycling pipeline with Thermal Dynamics pipes from reactor → pool drum → centrifuge for a hands-free fuel reprocessing loop!
